attributes.description: Alerts when `mongodb.connection.count (type=available) < 10`. Low available connections mean clients are about to hit the pool limit.
attributes.name: [MongoDB OTel] Connection exhaustion

investigation_guide.blob:
## MongoDB connection exhaustion

### What fired
Minimum `mongodb.connection.count` with `type=available` dropped below 10 during the window.

### Why it matters
Each Mongo host has a bounded connection pool. Running out means clients get connection errors and retry storms can overwhelm the server.

### Triage
1. Check client pool configuration.
2. Identify clients holding many idle connections.

### Remediation
- Raise the pool limit.
- Fix client-side connection leaks.

### Tuning
- Threshold 10; tune to your pool size.

esqlQuery.esql:
TS metrics-mongodbreceiver.otel-default
// Scope to MongoDB OTel metrics
| WHERE data_stream.dataset == "mongodbreceiver.otel"
| WHERE metrics.mongodb.connection.count IS NOT NULL AND attributes.type == "available"
// Minimum available connections per (server, database); then worst per server
| STATS avail = MIN(LAST_OVER_TIME(metrics.mongodb.connection.count)) BY resource.attributes.server.address, attributes.db.namespace
| STATS min_avail = MIN(avail) BY resource.attributes.server.address
// Alert when available connections drop below threshold (adjust 10 for your pool size)
| WHERE min_avail < 10
| SORT min_avail ASC
| LIMIT 20
